Just now

--

Reflect scores your own usage against 4 fluency skills across a 12-month window. It’s consumer-plan and memory-gated — here’s how a decision-maker should actually read it, and where the team numbers really live.
New here? The free AI Adoption ROI One-Pager is the one-page version of the “is this actually working” question this post is about — grab it, then read on.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Mohammad Rahmani on UnsplashOn July 9, Anthropic gave every Claude user a personal dashboard that scores their own AI habits against four named skills and looks back as far as 12 months. It’s called Reflect, it lives under Settings → Time and focus, and the press instantly labeled it “Claude Wrapped meets Apple Screen Time.”
If you run a team, that framing will set a trap. Reflect looks like the adoption dashboard you’ve been asking for — proof of who’s using AI, how, and whether it’s paying off. It isn’t. Reflect measures awareness, not adoption, and it does it for one person: you. The number that tells you what your team is doing, and what it costs, lives in a completely different place. Mixing those two up is how leaders end up “measuring” AI with a wellness gadget.
Here’s what Reflect actually is, why it’s easy to misread, and the one thing it’s genuinely good for.
What Reflect…
